name: pywinrm (Windows Remote Management)

auth.notes: Basic (plaintext — avoid in prod), NTLM, Kerberos, certificate auth. NTLM default for domain environments

performance.notes: Network-bound; WinRM connection overhead 100-500ms per session

agent_readiness.notes: Windows Remote Management (WS-Management) Python client. import winrm. s = winrm.Session('windows-host', auth=('user', 'pass'), transport='ntlm'). r = s.run_cmd('ipconfig'). r.std_out, r.std_err, r.status_code. PowerShell: r = s.run_ps('Get-Process'). winrm.Session(transport='kerberos') for AD. HTTPS: winrm.Session(server_cert_validation='ignore') for self-signed. run_cmd() for cmd.exe, run_ps() for PowerShell. Protocol.open_shell() for interactive. Error: WinRMTransportError. Requires WinRM enabled on target: winrm quickconfig.

gotchas: WinRM must be enabled on target Windows hosts — not on by default
Basic auth sends credentials in plaintext — always use HTTPS in production
Kerberos requires additional packages: kerberos, krb5 (platform-specific)
No type annotations — poor IDE support
NTLM over HTTP is vulnerable to relay attacks — prefer Kerberos
best_when: Automating Windows server management from Linux/Mac/Python
Ansible WinRM connections (Ansible uses pywinrm internally)
Running PowerShell scripts remotely on Windows hosts
Windows infrastructure automation in enterprise AD environments
avoid_when: When SSH is available (WinRM has worse tooling)
Basic auth over HTTP — security risk
When psexec or native tools suffice
summary: pywinrm enables Python automation of Windows hosts via WS-Management. winrm.Session('host', auth=('user','pass'), transport='ntlm').run_cmd('ipconfig'). run_ps() for PowerShell. NTLM/Kerberos/certificate auth. MIT by diyan. Best for Windows server automation and Ansible WinRM. WinRM must be pre-enabled on targets. Avoid Basic auth over HTTP. Kerberos preferred in AD environments. No type annotations.
